fragments.content: Pharmacogenomics studies how genetic variation affects drug response. Key genes: CYP2D6 (opioids, beta-blockers, SSRIs, tamoxifen), CYP2C19 (clopidogrel, PPIs, antidepressants), CYP2C9/VKORC1 (warfarin), TPMT/NUDT15 (thiopurines), UGT1A1 (irinotecan), SLCO1B1 (statin myopathy risk), HLA-B*1502 (carbamazepine Stevens-Johnson syndrome in Asians), HLA-B*5701 (abacavir hypersensitivity). Polymorphisms create metabolizer phenotypes: poor (no function), intermediate (reduced), extensive/normal (wild-type), ultrarapid (gene duplication/amplification). FDA includes pharmacogenomic information in >300 drug labels; CPIC (Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium) provides evidence-based dosing guidelines.

Clinical implementation examples: CYP2C19 poor metabolizers on clopidogrel have reduced active metabolite, higher cardiovascular event rates - use prasugrel or ticagrelor instead. CYP2D6 poor metabolizers: codeine/tramadol ineffective (need conversion to active metabolites); use morphine directly. CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizers: codeine toxicity risk (rapid conversion to morphine) - avoid entirely. TPMT deficiency: thiopurine (azathioprine, 6-MP) dose reduction 50-90% or alternative agent to prevent life-threatening myelosuppression. UGT1A1*28 homozygotes: irinotecan dose reduction 30% to prevent severe neutropenia/diarrhea. HLA-B*5701 screening mandatory before abacavir - positive = contraindicated. Warfarin: CYP2C9/VKORC1 genotyping guides initial dose but INR monitoring still required.

Genetic testing methodologies: PCR-based genotyping for known variants (rapid, inexpensive); next-generation sequencing for comprehensive panels; microarray chips for multiple variants simultaneously. Turnaround time varies: point-of-care tests 1-2 hours; commercial labs 3-14 days. Phenotype confirmation possible with probe drugs but rarely needed clinically. Allele nomenclature standardized: *1 = wild-type/function; *2-*9 etc. = variant alleles with defined functional impact. CPIC levels evidence: Level A (high) - gene-drug pairs with actionable recommendations (CYP2C19-clopidogrel, TPMT-thiopurines); Level B (moderate) - potential actionability. Preemptive genotyping (panel testing before prescribing) increasingly used in health systems. Insurance coverage variable; cost-effectiveness demonstrated for several gene-drug pairs.
